title: payment — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for creating payment intents, tokenizing methods, and reading provider capabilities. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic. Callers who need the wire-shape of intents use the PaymentIntentOrchestrator + PaymentIntentData path; helpers here are for imperative flows (support tools, tests, ad-hoc scripts).

helpers.purpose: Imperative wrapper for PaymentIntentOrchestrator::createIntent. Uses the tenant's default provider when $providerSlug is null + the customer's default method when $method is null. Returns the created PaymentIntent (with client_secret populated for customer-side SDK confirmation).

Returns the driver instance for a provider config row. Delegates to PaymentProviderManager::forConfig() which reads $config->provider + $config->config and calls the appropriate createXxxDriver factory. Reserved for advanced consumers — typical flows go through PaymentIntentOrchestrator.

Returns the active provider_configs for (tenant, currency, country_code) — filters by is_active=true + supports the currency + supports the region (per provider capability matrix). Used by PaymentIntentOrchestrator to pick a provider when the caller doesn't specify.

Wrapper over ThreeDsGate::requires. Reads tenant 3DS2 threshold + PSD2 regime + force_3ds2 entitlement + high-risk region config. Returns true when the transaction requires 3DS2 challenge. Used by PaymentIntentOrchestrator to set next_action_type at intent create.
Hashes a raw device fingerprint (from Stripe.js / Adyen.js / provider SDK) with SHA-256. Returns 64-character lowercase hex. NEVER store the raw fingerprint — it's PII.
Hashes a provider's card fingerprint (returned by Stripe as fingerprint, by Adyen as fingerprint, etc.) with SHA-256. Used by PaymentMethodObserver to deduplicate saved methods per customer.
Encrypts a provider reference (pm_..., pi_..., etc.) at rest via TokenEncryptor (AES-256-GCM). Returns encrypted ciphertext with key_version prefix.
Decrypts a provider reference. Reads key_version prefix to select the correct decryption key (supports rotation). Used by the driver factory only — never in general application code.

Verifies HMAC-SHA256 (or provider-specific scheme) signature over the webhook body + timestamp. Delegates to per-provider verifiers. Used by the payment.webhook_verify middleware. Returns true only when signature verifies + timestamp within tolerance window.

Computes the provider fee for a given payment. Reads per-provider fee schedule from data/provider-capability-matrix.json (fees_by_method_type). Used at Payment.create time to populate fee_cents. Returns fee in cents.
Returns true when the card is expired (exp_year < current_year, OR exp_year == current_year AND exp_month < current_month). Used by PaymentMethod.is_expired accessor + SyncPaymentMethodExpiryJob.

Auto-collects supporting evidence for a dispute from other modules. Returns an evidence array shaped as { customer_signature?, receipt?, service_documentation?, product_description?, refund_policy?, communication_log?, uncategorized_text? }. Used by CollectDisputeEvidenceJob.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.

The create_payment_intent helper is intentionally the primary imperative capture path. For most use cases (auto-triggered by invoice finalization), the CreatePaymentIntentOnInvoiceFinalized hook path is preferred over calling this directly.

The encrypt_provider_reference / decrypt_provider_reference helpers are intentionally NOT called from general application code — the observer chain handles encryption/decryption automatically via the Eloquent 'encrypted:string' cast. Direct calls are reserved for driver factories reading credentials at runtime + support tools.
The is_card_expired helper reads the current UTC date/time — timezone-sensitive comparisons should not use this helper (there is no 'card expired in EST' — expiry is absolute).
